name: devcontainer-dev-certs-host
description: Generates and trusts ASP.NET and Aspire compatible HTTPS development certificates on the host machine for use in Dev Containers and remote environments.

commands.title: Dev Certs: Trust Certificate in Browsers

devcontainer-dev-certs.autoProvision.description: Allow certificate provisioning when the workspace extension requests one. On first use, a consent prompt explains what will happen before any certificates are generated. Set to false to disable provisioning entirely.

devcontainerDevCerts.generateDotNetCert.description: Auto-generate the ASP.NET Core / Aspire compatible HTTPS development certificate and trust it in the host OS store. When false, user-managed certificates (if any) are still synced but no dev cert is generated.

devcontainerDevCerts.userCertificates.description: User-managed certificates to sync from the host into dev containers. Each entry supplies exactly one of pfxPath (+ optional pfxPassword) or pemCertPath (+ optional pemKeyPath). Container-side filenames derive from the entry's 'name'. User-managed certs are never added to the host OS trust store.

name.patternErrorMessage: Must be 1-64 characters from [A-Za-z0-9._-], with no leading dot (rejects '.', '..', and hidden-file names).
name.description: Stable basename used for files inside the container (e.g. '${name}.pem'). Must be 1-64 chars from [A-Za-z0-9._-] with no leading dot.

pfxPath.description: Absolute path on the host to a PFX/PKCS#12 file.

pfxPassword.description: Password for the PFX, if any. Leave unset for an empty password.

pemCertPath.description: Absolute path on the host to a PEM-encoded certificate.

pemKeyPath.description: Absolute path on the host to a PEM-encoded private key. Omit for CA-only certs — the cert will still be trusted in the container but no private key will be synced.

trustInContainer.description: When true (default), plant the cert in the OpenSSL trust directory and the .NET Root store inside the container.
